title: Podman container exits immediately when SELinux is set to enforcing
body:
### Issue Description

With SELinux set to enforcing, any container started with Podman (using a rootless user) exits immediately without any error message.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. On Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.1, set SELinux enforcing
2. Run `podman run -it ubi8 bash` (or any other container like busybox)
3. No logs are output, no errors are written, the container just exits


### Describe the results you received

No logs are output, no errors are written, the container just exits.

### Describe the results you expected

The container should start and open bash.

Alternatively, it should give a helpful error message why the container could not start.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Using a RHEL 9.1 VM in vSphere.

### Additional information

`sudo yum --showduplicates list podman` does not include 4.4.2, only 4.2.0, so that is why I haven't tested with 4.4.2.

Note also that `/home/ansible/.config/containers/storage.conf` does not exist, nor does `/home/ansible/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes`. The folder `/home/ansible/.local/share/containers/storage` does exist.

comments.body: What AVC messaes are you seeing?

Does `sudo yum -y reinstall container-selinux` throw an error message.

You might need to downgrade container-selinux.

The strange thing was that there were also no AVC messages.

Today, I recreated my Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 VM, and the problem was suddenly solved. I think I have logged this issue incorrectly on version 9.1, that it was 9.0 that I could reproduce it on. It may also be that the fresh install of podman on this new VM solved it (yum install podman). Anyways, I'm closing the issue.
